title: Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 4

'''''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 4''' is the fourth issue in the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith|''Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'']] series of comics. It was published by [[Dark Horse Comics]] on [[January 10]], [[1995]].

==Opening crawl==
{{OpeningCrawl
|name=Death of a Dark Jedi
|text=Following a disastrous confrontation with the malevolent Krath, Jedi Knights from all over the Galaxy come together on the planet Deneba to deal with this new threat to peace and justice. Ulic Qel-Droma makes a dangerous proposal: he will infiltrate the Krath and learn the secrets of their dark-side power.

As Ulic's own Master Arca and others protest his plan, assassin droids sent by the Krath unleash deadly weaponry, killing many brave Jedi before they can be destroyed. Among the fallen is the great Master Arca himself.

As Ulic mourns his teacher, Nomi Sunrider warns Ulic Qel-Droma that it is foolish to think he can eradicate the dark side by learning its ways. But Ulic Qel-Droma vows to succeed where others have failed: "I will do this for Master Arca... for all the Jedi Knights... and for the good of the Republic!"

Few among the great Jedi gathered on Deneba have foreseen the far greater disturbance that is about to divide the Galaxy.

On the planet Korriban, the wandering Jedi Exar Kun discovers a valley of spectacular Sith tombs. There, the spirits of the ancient Dark Lords of the Sith test their new protege.

As alchemical Guardian creatures attack him, Kun finds his light-side powers mysteriously blocked. The only way Exar Kun can defeat the monsters is to release the power of anger, which bursts through him with great force.

Now, guided by the dark spirit of Freedon Nadd, Exar Kun sets out to retrieve ancient Sith power objects on a remote jungle moon...
}}

==Plot summary==
===Journey to Yavin 4===
Following the trail of [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]], [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]] arrives at the [[Yavin 4/Legends|fourth moon of Yavin]] and is greeted by the [[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]]. [[Freedon Nadd]]'s [[Force ghost/Legends|spirit]] is still present, and he warns Kun of the Massassi. Kun does not heed Nadd's warnings and is captured. Suddenly, the Massassi priest [[Zythmnr]] emerges from his solitude, claims Kun is the one they have waited for, and delivers him to the [[Temple of Fire]].

===Ulic's quest for vengeance===
On [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]], [[Jedi Master/Legends|Master]] [[Ood Bnar]] gives [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]] the [[Definitive history of the Empress Teta system|definitive history]] of the [[Empress Teta system/Legends|Empress Teta system]] to prepare for his mission to infiltrate the [[Krath/Legends|Krath]]. Ulic's friend [[Nomi Sunrider]] and his brother [[Cay Qel-Droma]] try to reason with Ulic but he is stubborn. Before Ulic departs, he is brought before Master [[Odan-Urr/Legends|Odan-Urr]], who uses a [[Sith holocron/Legends|Sith holocron]] to show him an example of a [[Unidentified Jedi Knight (Dark Holocron)|Jedi who wished to conquer the dark side from within]], but failed and was seduced by the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]].

Ulic is offended by Odan-Urr's disapproval, and goes on to claim the he must do this. As Ulic departs he is stopped by his brother [[Cay Qel-Droma|Cay]], who confesses his concerns for this mission and his worry that he'll never see his brother again. Ulic shrugs the idea off, and departs for [[Cinnagar/Legends|Cinnagar]] disguised as a smuggler.

===The Massassi temple===
In the heart of the [[Temple of Fire]] on Yavin 4, Kun is the center of a Massassi blood sacrifice. Rather than being afraid, Kun believes that he has finally reached the knowledge he seeks. As the ritual goes on, an amazing amount of power is released and a [[Sith wyrm|massive beast]] created from [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow's]] [[Sith alchemy/Legends|dark side alchemy]] awakens.

===Undercover on Cinnagar===
Now on the surface of Cinnagar, Ulic begins his mission. Unbeknownst to him, [[Satal Keto/Legends|Satal]] and [[Aleema Keto]] have already discovered his presence and plan to manipulate him. The next day, the Krath hold a public execution for several rebellious [[Carbonite/Legends|carbonite]] traders, who are hurled into a carbon smelter.

However, several rebellious carbonite miners take the opportunity to stage an attack on Aleema
at a public execution, a sudden ambush against the Krath is staged by rebels. Seeking to gain the trust of the Krath, Ulic intervenes in order to learn the dark side secrets of Aleema and Satal. Ulic swiftly dispatches the rebels with his lightsaber. Having won the trust of the Krath, Aleema welcomes Ulic but her cousin Satal is jealous of the newcomer.

===Embracing darkness===
Facing certain death at the hand of [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Sadow's]] creature on Yavin 4, Kun embraces the power of the dark side yet again and takes control of an ancient [[Sith talisman/Legends|Sith amulet]]. Channeling the power of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] through this amulet, Kun incinerates the beast with his Force powers. Impressed by his mastery of the Force, the Massassi accept him as their new leader.

Pleased with Exar Kun's journey to the dark side, Nadd begins planning to resurrect himself using Naga Sadow's laboratory. However, Kun has had enough of his Sith master and uses the amulet's power to forever vanquish the spirit of Freedon Nadd. In his final moments, Nadd warns Satal and Aleema to beware of the "pretender" Exar Kun and to defend the legacy of the Sith.

==Continuity==
There is an artistic continuity error in the three panels where Ulic and Cay discuss his departure, right before their hug: the middle panel supposedly shows Ulic's face, however he is in the same position where his brother's face is, and the speech bubble with his lines point to the other side of the panel.
